returnInterest.note: Definitely want to come back to see more of the country.

description.intro: Spain offers a good combination of lively cities, rich history, excellent food culture, and a very relaxed atmosphere, making it perfect for both exploring and relaxing.
pros: Excellent food culture with many regional specialties
Very affordable compared to many western European countries
culturalNotes: Dinner time is very late; typically between 21:00 and 23:00
Nightlife starts late; people often go out to party around 01:00 or later

paymentCulture: Both cash and card payments are widely accepted
transportTips: You can easily travel between major cities by train
Book long-distance train tickets in advance for better prices
Spain’s high-speed AVE network is fast and convenient for transportation between major cities
Connections between smaller cities are often very slow
You usually have to pass through a security check before accessing high-speed train platforms
languageTips: Learning basic Spanish makes getting around much easier
English is widely spoken in major cities, but less common in smaller towns
Regional languages such as Catalan and Valencian are commonly used alongside Spanish in some areas

notes: Late winter (around February) had very comfortable temperatures, especially along the east coast.

foodNotes: There are many tapas bars where you can share small plates of different dishes—definitely worth trying.
Many restaurants offer a daily set menu (menú del día), and sometimes even breakfast menus, which are usually very affordable.

regionsOverview.name: València
notes: Besides Spanish, Valencian (valencià), a dialect of Catalan, is widely spoken.

recommendedApps.name: Renfe
recommendedApps.purpose: The app of the national train service.
